index.title: Accounts
index.description: View, create, edit, and delete your accounts.
index.scope: Only accounts belonging to the signed-in user are shown.
index.empty: No accounts yet. Add your first account to get started.

typeDescriptions.credit_card: Card purchases are expenses, while card payments are transfers, so this balance approximates the outstanding amount.
typeDescriptions.e_money: Payment purchases are expenses, while billing reallocations and top-ups are transfers, so this balance represents available funds or pending settlement.
typeDescriptions.other: When used for payment or billing management, this balance represents available funds or pending settlement.
typeDescriptions.securities: Contributions and fund movements into securities accounts are transfers and are not counted twice in income or expenses.

form.openingBalanceHint: The opening balance is treated as the balance before transactions on this date. If left blank, it is the starting point for the entire history.
form.activeHint: Inactive accounts remain visible with their status clearly marked.
form.balanceSectionDescription: Set the account’s role in net worth and how its balance is calculated separately from its account type.
form.clearingHint: Clearing accounts track billing and funding routes and are excluded from net worth.
form.snapshotHint: Securities use their valuation as the baseline, adjusted only by cash flows after the valuation date.
form.netWorthHint: Turn this off for development accounts or accounts you want to exclude from totals.
form.monthlyCloseHint: Include accounts with delayed updates—such as card bills, bank balances, e-money, points, and fund valuations—in the month-end checklist.
form.importAliasesHint: Add one alias per line for names that appear in Money Forward transfer descriptions or merchant names. Example: MasterCard(8658)

reconciliation.title: Balance Reconciliation
reconciliation.description: Compare official balances with ledger balances and adjust opening balances by the difference.
reconciliation.confirmAdjustment: Apply the {{difference}} {{currency}} difference to the opening balance of {{name}}?
reconciliation.notePlaceholder: Confirmed that imports are up to date
reconciliation.warningDescription: Import all transactions through the target date before entering the financial institution balance. If transactions are missing, their amounts will be included in the opening balance adjustment.
reconciliation.ledgerAccountsDescription: Enter assets as positive actual balances and liabilities such as credit cards as negative outstanding balances.
reconciliation.valuationAccountsDescription: Record same-day market values for securities accounts instead of adjusting their opening balances.
reconciliation.clearingAccountsDescription: Clearing accounts do not have actual balances and are not adjusted. Investigate billing or funding routes when their balances become large.

snapshots.title: {{name}} Valuations
snapshots.description: Record the daily market value of a securities account.
snapshots.currentWithSnapshot: Latest valuation plus subsequent cash flows
snapshots.currentWithoutSnapshot: No valuation recorded; showing ledger balance
snapshots.methodWarning: Change the account’s balance calculation method to “Valuation Snapshot” before recording valuations.
snapshots.missingWarning: The current value is the cumulative cash flow, not the actual market value. Recording the first valuation makes it the new balance baseline.
snapshots.formDescription: Recording another valuation for the same date updates that day’s value. Valuations are treated as balances after that day’s transactions.
